Now let's move from the main body of the script onto the clothes. Now the first thing to remember with the clothes is that we always start with a transitional pattern which explains exactly how we get the buying process going. An example would be this. Now John, getting started is very simple. Just a question of some basic information to get the accounts up and then we handle everything else for you. That's your basic transition. I'll repeat it again. Now John, getting started is very simple. Or John, opening up an account is very simple. Just a question of your name, some basic information. And we can do this in three easy payments, whatever the actual specifics of your industry are. Okay? And we do everything else for you. That's your transition. So after you say, you know, the company's amazing, the product's amazing, you say, now John, getting started is very simple. That's your transition into your clothes. And remember, this is a very, very important psychological trigger to say that something is easy to get started, no big deal. This is an equation that every single person runs before they make a decision. And it's called energy in and benefits out. Essentially, how much energy do I have to expend now to get some benefits in the future? So if you set up the situation, here's how you do it. There's going to be so much paperwork, John. It's going to cost you a lot of money right now. It's going to be a hassle to put this thing together when you get home. And then down the road, you'll feel good. He's probably not going to take action. Versus you saying, now John, getting started is very simple. Just a question of some few basic things. And we handle everything else for you. Then you need to get this, that, that wonderful benefits after. And you ask for the order elegantly and say, believe me, you'll be very impressed. He's probably going to say yes. Okay? So your tone here. Remember, it must be congruent with the language pattern. In other words, when you say, now John, getting started is very, very simple. Okay? They'll be like, what the hell is wrong with this guy? It's calmness. Now John, getting started is very, very simple. That's the no big deal tone. Okay? Just a couple of quick questions. You'll see some basic information to open up the account. And we handle everything else for you. Now that's a very powerful closing pattern. And it really helps someone say, you know what? It's kind of really easy to get started here. And there's so many great, wonderful benefits I'm thinking about right now. They're playing that movie out and bam, people say, all right, let's do it. Okay? So that's your powerful closing transition when you ask for the order. Okay? Now before I move on, there's one important point I want to make here. And that's sometimes you are in a situation where the process or the product that you have actually to get started is not all that simple. So you couldn't just say, you know, something's very simple when it's actually complicated. Some examples will be where like in banking or in mortgages, there's definitely some hoops that have to be jumped through and some paperwork to be filled out. But still, even in spite of that, you can make it clear to your client that you'll do your very best to make this as simple as possible. You know what I'm saying? Listen, we make this very, very simple for you as much as possible. We'll handle all the paperwork and we'll hold your hand every step of the way, so on and so forth. So again, it's not about misleading the client to make a complicated situation. We'll say it's really simple and he finds out it's complicated. That's not what I'm saying. What I'm saying is that even if the situation is complicated, you can still say, John, there's some steps we got to go through, but you know, we handle all the paperwork for you and you calm him down through that sympathetic and pathetic tone.
